markdown:
You are tidying the Archon Horizon **memory inbox** for the JacobianChallenge workspace. Invoke the CLI via `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox ...` (the env var is an absolute path). Run `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` to read current state.

**Situation**: there are 29 open `memory`-kind items; the target is roughly 10–12. Your job is to `archive` (soft-delete, not `delete`) items whose recorded work is **closed/consumed** or **superseded by a strictly-later item on the same topic**, while preserving one representative of each distinct still-useful recipe and ALL active-blocker context.

**PROTECTED — never archive these** (active-cone blockers / current / general conventions):
- I-0089 (T12/QuotScheme current state), I-0088 (ModuleCat-R morphism recipe, used this round)
- I-0086 (T12/AJC.picrep triage), I-0083 (FBC naturality leaves — open T2), I-0076 (pullback_preservesFiniteLimits — open T2)
- I-0082 (A.3 Pic0 cone state — open), I-0061 (tangentSpaceIso critical path — open), I-0074 (FGA picSharp caveats — open foundation)
- I-0065 (Milne 3.1/3.2 FALSE-chain dead-end — must-not-redo), I-0051 (leandag audit convention — durable general)
- I-0087 (issue) and I-0091 (info) are NOT memory items — ignore them entirely.

**Archive criteria** (apply judgment; an item must clearly meet one):
1. Its recorded proof/work is fully closed AND it is superseded by a later item covering the same ground (e.g. an older FBC/bareBC or v4.31-migration note whose content a newer note restates). A strong example: **I-0021** (FBC bareBC Stage-2, session 0003) is superseded by **I-0034** (session 0007 essImage route that closed `IsIso bareBC`) — archive I-0021.
2. It records a one-off ops/build-timing observation already generalized by a later ops note (e.g. an older "build-wall hours" note generalized by a later "build cost is a load artifact / check uptime" note).
3. It is a closed-work recipe on a subproject leg that is fully done AND whose specific route has no plausible forward reuse for remaining active work.

**Do NOT archive** distinct hard-won recipes that plausibly aid remaining active legs (Albanese Milne-3.3 / codim-1 / Pic^0 tangent-space, the T2/FBC flatness algebra, the QuotScheme reps endgame) even if the specific proof they describe is closed — those are durable knowledge, not consumed context.

For each item you archive: run `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox archive <ID>` and note the one-line reason. Aim to land around 10–14 open memory items. When done, report: the list of archived IDs each with its ≤12-word reason, and the final open-memory count. Do not add or edit any items; archive only.
